structural_claim: Universal scaling laws govern system behavior near critical transitions, making microscopic details irrelevant and organizing systems into universal classes

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Near phase transitions, systems exhibit scale invariance and fall into universal classes independent of microscopic details
Turbulent flows exhibit universal power-law scaling in the inertial range, independent of specific flow conditions
Flows organize into similarity classes based on dimensionless parameters, with universal behavior within each class
